members.name: Dianne Avey
members.bio: For over forty years Dianne Avey has worked in a variety of health care settings. She earned her undergraduate and graduate degrees from the University of Washington. Starting her career as an RN in Critical Care, she learned important skills that she used to further her career as she went on to work in Community Health, Hospice, and teaching at both Tacoma Community College and the University of Washington. Dianne returned to the UofW and completed her training as a Family Nurse Practitioner in 1990. She has worked in this role for 36yrs at a variety of primary care clinics in the Northwest. She is a long-time Island resident and a descendant of one of the pioneer families – the Ekenstams. Retiring from full-time clinical practice in 2026, she is looking forward to continuing to serve her community and use her skills as a member of the board for Anderson Island Healthcare Advocates.

members.name: Glenn Kasman
members.bio: I have been an Anderson Island resident since 2016 and an AIHA board member since 2023. I am a retired healthcare executive, having served as regional President for a major provider of inpatient and outpatient services in Washington State. My portfolio included leadership of hospital, clinic, physician, and health system operations as well as community relations, strategic planning and business development transactions. I began my career as a physical therapist, authored professional publications, and presented extensively in national and international settings. I have enjoyed volunteer service with local civic groups as well as professional societies, associations, and foundations with regional, national, and international reach.

members.name: Jane Fowler
members.bio: I moved to Anderson Island in 2025 and immediately felt at home here. I am a retired nurse practitioner with nearly two decades of experience working with underserved and rural communities in my home state of Wisconsin. Before that I practiced corporate law for 20+ years. I've served on the boards of several nongovernment organizations. The island's strong sense of community truly inspires me. I hope to make a positive difference in my current role as president of AIHA.

members.name: Michelle Ames
members.bio: With a background that blends 30 years of design with two decades of health and wellness, I've always believed that our environments and our bodies should work in harmony. As a Certified Pilates Trainer for the last 20 years I've had the privilege of helping people through restorative movement, particularly those facing neurological conditions and Scoliosis. Today, I'm leaning into my role as a connector for the Anderson Island Community Fitness Center and the Health Care Advocates. By volunteering within both of these community organizations, we will make great strides in bringing easier access to varied healthcare services for all of our neighbors.
